channel.title: NYT > World > Middle East
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: U.S. Officials Believed Israel Was Plotting to Kill Iranian Negotiators
item.description: Any Israeli attempt to kill Abbas Araghchi, the foreign minister, or Mohammad Ghalibaf, the Parliament speaker, would have derailed peace talks, American officials feared.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes and Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, left, with Abbas Araghchi, center, traveled to Switzerland in June for a second in-person meeting with Vice President JD Vance and the American delegation.

item.title: Bomb Explodes at Damascus Cafe, Killing at Least 6
item.description: The blast, near the Syrian capital’s main courthouse, underscored the country’s persistent instability as its leaders try to chart a path out of years of dictatorship and war.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward and Reham Mourshed
item.category: Islamic State in Iraq and Syria (ISIS)
item.media:description: Syrian security forces at the site of an explosion at a cafe in Damascus on Thursday.

item.title: Iran Prepares to Bury Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Killed at Iran War’s Outset
item.description: Long-delayed funeral ceremonies for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, killed during U.S.-Israeli strikes at the war’s outset, are set to begin Friday. For the regime, it is a critical moment to demonstrate that it has endured.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: Workers in Tehran assembled scaffolding on Thursday as the country prepared for the funeral of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, whose image adorned the side of a nearby building.

item.title: U.S. Hopes for Investment in Iran Would Break With Decades of History
item.description: Senior U.S. officials have said Iran would be richly rewarded for changing its stance on the United States. Iran’s leaders have rejected such a bargain in the past.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Grand Bazaar in Iran’s capital, Tehran, in May. The U.S. has offered Iran sanctions relief if it agrees to limits on its nuclear program.

item.title: U.S. Restarts Shipments of Dollars to Iraq, Pausing Pressure
item.description: The Trump administration had halted the deliveries as part of its efforts to pressure the Baghdad government to distance itself from Iran.
item.dc:creator: Raja Abdulrahim and Falih Hassan
item.media:description: Iraq’s prime minister, Ali al-Zaidi, has taken steps to try to rein in Iran-backed militias in his country.

item.title: How the Iran War Ignited a Clash Between Trump and the Saudi Crown Prince
item.description: Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman pressed President Trump earlier to cripple Iran. But as Iran asserted its power, the prince urged a cease-fire, and is now pursuing his security priorities.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump with Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman of Saudi Arabia in Washington last year. The two countries have become increasingly at odds over the U.S. war with Iran.

item.title: U.S. and Iran Set to Hold Indirect Talks After Trading Attacks: What to Know
item.description: Negotiators are in Qatar for indirect talks and ship traffic has risen in the Strait of Hormuz, even as Tehran and Washington remain far apart on key issues. Here’s what to know.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Jared Kushner and Steve Witkoff, advisers to President Trump, in Islamabad, Pakistan, in April. They met with Qatar’s prime minister on Tuesday to discuss the U.S.-Iran deal.

item.title: Trump Suggestion of a Syrian Crackdown on Hezbollah Confounds Many in Mideast
item.description: During peace talks, President Trump repeatedly floated the idea that Syria could help subdue Hezbollah in Lebanon. The proposal revived bitter memories.
item.dc:creator: Christina Goldbaum and Raja Abdulrahim
item.media:description: A man in the village of Nabatieh al Fawka, Lebanon. He returned earlier this week after fleeing heavy fighting between Israeli forces and Hezbollah in the area.

item.title: Turkey as a Tango Destination? A Reporter Was Living That Story.
item.description: A Times journalist profiled Istanbul’s thriving tango scene after finding herself transformed by the dance.
item.dc:creator: Safak Timur
item.media:credit: Laura Boushnak for The New York Times
item.media:description: Safak Timur, a reporter in The Times’s Istanbul bureau, dancing at a tango school in Istanbul. Aficionados say they can identify Turks by their dancing.

item.title: With New Seizures, Value of the Met’s Looted Artifacts Tops $95 Million
item.description: The Manhattan district attorney’s office said it secured the return of dozens of ancient artifacts in June, after a process that the museum described as collaborative.
item.dc:creator: Graham Bowley and Tom Mashberg

item.title: After U.S.-Iran War, Oman Is Said to Propose Strait of Hormuz Fee Plan
item.description: Under the proposal, Iran and Oman would collect payment for ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz, according to officials and diplomats with knowledge of the matter.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim, Farnaz Fassihi and Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A cargo vessel anchored off the coast of Oman near Muscat last week.

item.title: The 1968 Pact on Shipping Routes in the Strait of Hormuz That Iran Is Rejecting
item.description: For decades, a maritime agreement negotiated by Iran and Oman has governed the main pathways through the strait.
item.dc:creator: John Yoon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A vessel in the Strait of Hormuz. A 1968 agreement was largely a technical solution to prevent collisions between supertankers there.

item.title: Shipping Traffic in Strait of Hormuz Picks Up Again
item.description: Vessels stranded for months have started moving in larger numbers, but many pulled back over the weekend after Iran and the U.S. exchanged attacks.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gross
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Stocks Notch Strongest Quarter Since 2020
item.description: The S&P 500 rose almost 15 percent for the three months through June, and many stock analysts remain optimistic that corporate earnings driven by artificial intelligence will keep growing.
item.dc:creator: Joe Rennison
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Leo Sands, Aaron Boxerman and Hari Raj

item.title: Abdul Ahad Momand, Only Afghan to Fly in Space, Is Dead
item.description: A pilot in the Afghan Air Force, he was recruited for the Soviet space program. His 1988 flight took place as the Soviets were fighting a war in his country.
item.dc:creator: Richard Sandomir

item.title: Ships Pull Back Amid Heightened Risk From U.S.-Iran Strikes
item.description: Four days of attacks by both countries forced a sharp decline in the number of vessels navigating through the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gross
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Shirin Hakim, Adam Rasgon, Natan Odenheimer, Elian Peltier and Aaron Boxerman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Helicopter Crash Near Major Saudi Oil Refinery Kills 14 People
item.description: The incident occurred near the Ras Tanura facility in the east of the country, a key export hub for oil transported through the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim
item.media:description: Parts of the Ras Tanura oil refinery were closed in March after a fire following an Iranian drone attack.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman, Euan Ward, John Ismay, Tyler Pager and Ephrat Livni

item.title: What Life Is Like in Dahiya Amid a Hezbollah-Israel Truce in Lebanon
item.description: Dahiya, a sprawling area and Hezbollah stronghold, is stirring back to life amid a tense truce with Israel. “This war has brought so much loss,” one resident said.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir and David Guttenfelder
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A main street in Dahiya, a collection of neighborhoods south of Beirut, the Lebanese capital, in June. On any given day, Dahiya hums with bustling city rhythms.

item.title: Deal With Israel Divides Lebanese, Fueling Protests in Beirut
item.description: Supporters welcomed the agreement as a step toward curtailing Iran’s influence within Lebanon, but others took to the streets, calling it a capitulation.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: People in Beirut protesting against the agreement that was signed late Friday between Israel, Lebanon and the United States.

item.title: Once He Wanted to Lead FIFA. This Is Much Better.
item.description: Jordan’s arrival at the World Cup is the culmination of decades of patient effort by one man, but also a moment of solidarity for his nation and its diaspora.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja
item.category: Ali bin al-Hussein, Prince of Jordan
item.media:description: Prince Ali bin Al Hussein, center, ahead of a soccer game between the Jordanian and Palestinian teams in 2023.

item.title: In Strait of Hormuz, Renewed Strikes Threaten to Set Back Shipping Recovery
item.description: The attacks came after traffic through the Strait of Hormuz had reached the highest levels since the start of the U.S. war in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Peter Eavis and Jenny Gross
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Iran has tried to establish formal control over shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, something it did not have before.

item.title: U.S.-Iran Deal’s Vague Language Comes Back to Haunt Peace Efforts
item.description: The deal called for Iran to “make arrangements” for the passage of ships through the Strait of Hormuz. Iran has interpreted that to mean it can designate which routes ships take.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, as seen from Oman. Iran on Thursday struck a container ship passing through the waterway.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman, Euan Ward, Ismaeel Naar and Farnaz Fassihi

item.title: After New York Elections, Israelis Fear Further Rupture in U.S.-Israeli Relationship
item.description: Criticism of Israel’s war in Gaza, anger over the Iran war and election results in New York all suggest Israel’s solid support from Washington may be on borrowed time.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Angelina Katsanis for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani of New York, center, in Manhattan on Tuesday. Three pro-Palestinian candidates backed by Mr. Mamdani, an ardent critic of Israel, defeated moderates in Democratic congressional primaries this week.

item.title: At World Cup, Iran-Egypt ‘Pride Match’ Stirs Conversation About Persecution
item.description: Seattle had designated Friday’s match to celebrate L.G.B.T.Q. rights, but the teams that faced off are from countries that penalize homosexuality.
item.dc:creator: Tim Arango
item.media:description: Fans inside the stadium in Seattle holding up a Progress Pride flag during the Egypt and Iran match on Friday.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Helene Cooper, Euan Ward, Jenny Gross, Pranav Baskar and Alan Yuhas

item.title: Why Lebanon Is Celebrating Brazil’s Win
item.description: Our correspondent Abdi Latif Dahir visits a Brazil national team World Cup watch party in Tripoli, Lebanon. Brazil is especially popular here as it’s home to the world’s largest Lebanese diaspora.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir, Christina Thornell and Coleman Lowndes

item.title: Oil Prices Return to Prewar Levels After Four Months
item.description: The cost of crude has become a real-time barometer of the Iran war’s toll on the global economy.
item.dc:creator: Gregory Schmidt, Rebecca F. Elliott and Lisa Friedman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: U.S. Strikes Iran in Retaliation for Attack on Vessel in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Trump on Friday called Iran’s attack on a container ship transiting the Strait of Hormuz a day earlier a “foolish” act.
item.dc:creator: Helene Cooper, Euan Ward, Jenny Gross and Pranav Baskar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Oil tankers and cargo vessels anchored off the coast of Oman this week.

item.title: Iran’s Long World Cup Journey Defined by War and Political Tension
item.description: A win or draw against Egypt on Friday, and the team’s unusual journey will continue.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja and Gabriela Bhaskar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: How Three Months of War Changed the Gulf Forever
item.description: Facing an emerging U.S.-Iran deal that leaves them vulnerable, Gulf Arab countries are reshaping their defense strategies, economies and trade routes.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Omanis around Qaboos Port in Muscat, where oil tankers, carriers, vessels and Omani fishing boats were sitting anchored this month.

item.title: How a Jet Fuel Refinery Is Scrambling After the Iran War Oil Shock
item.description: For a mega-refinery in Ulsan, South Korea, a top exporter of jet fuel to the West Coast of the United States and other places, weaning off Middle Eastern oil is no small feat.
item.dc:creator: John Yoon and Chang W. Lee
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Chang W. Lee/The New York Times
